query: What sanctions can the Central Bank impose?
answer:
The Central Bank may impose the following sanction: cancellation of authorization for taking deposits as a bank, where it has open high-risk observations on expected credit loss models or insufficient assurance of their reliability/accuracy due to deficiencies in compliance with CBUAE Model Management standards or accounting standards [2 9.22].

Uncertainty: No other sanction types—such as fines or remedial directives—are identified in the provided clauses.

evidence.title: Determining provisions in the context of Islamic Financial Services may impact the
evidence.text:
Investment Account holders’ profit as provisions are deducted from profit. Consequently, LFIs
performing Islamic Financial Services must ensure that the Internal Shari’ah Supervision
Committee approves the provisioning policies. In addition, the determination of provisions
must be approved by the committee responsible for profit and loss allocation in accordance
with the Central Bank’s standard regarding profit equalization for Islamic Banks.
